title: rosa × dumetorum subsp. redunca
intro: Rosa × dumetorum subsp. redunca, also known as the Scotch rose or wild rose, is a subspecies of flowering plant belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae) and is native to the western Mediterranean region. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which can aid in the treatment of various health issues such as wounds, fever, and digestive problems. The health benefits of Rosa × dumetorum subsp. redunca include its ability to promote healthy skin, reduce stress and anxiety, and support immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and salves, which can be made by infusing the flowers and leaves in hot water or oil, and are often used to soothe skin irritations and promote wound healing.
